core_mechanism: threshold-gated collective activation with an every-prefix feasibility constraint: units switch on only once a critical mass is committed, and requiring each intermediate partial set to independently exceed the threshold strands an observable incremental process at a suboptimal stable set, whereas hiding commitments until quorum (escrow) lets the system reach the maximal feasible set by tunneling through the otherwise-infeasible intermediate region
brief_reason: The abstract casts safe organizing as a cascade of speaking up that must stay safe at every step and so stops short of the largest safe group, while private-until-enough-join assurance contracts 'tunnel' to that largest group, i.e. a critical-mass activation threshold plus a path-feasibility constraint that hidden commitment circumvents.
